artifact: Masnavi — the reed-flute laments separation from its reed-bed.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — the reed-flute laments separation from its reed-bed.
english: The song begins where the soul feels its distance from home.
spanish: El canto empieza donde el alma siente su distancia del hogar.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — the flute's cry is fire, not air.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — the flute's cry is fire, not air.
english: A living teaching burns before it explains.
spanish: Una enseñanza viva arde antes de explicar.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — love possesses the wine and inspires the flute.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — love possesses the wine and inspires the flute.
english: What moves the song is the same ferment that changes the cup.
spanish: Lo que mueve el canto es el mismo fermento que transforma la copa.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — the raw cannot understand the ripe.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — the raw cannot understand the ripe.
english: Not every ear is ready for what the fire has cooked.
spanish: No todo oído está listo para lo que el fuego ha cocido.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — arise and burst the bonds of silver and gold.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — arise and burst the bonds of silver and gold.
english: Freedom starts where possession stops being the measure of life.
spanish: La libertad empieza donde la posesión deja de medir la vida.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — the ocean poured into a pitcher holds only a day's store.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — the ocean poured into a pitcher holds only a day's store.
english: The vessel of desire cannot contain the sea it wants.
spanish: La vasija del deseo no puede contener el mar que pretende.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — the shell fills with pearls only when content.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — the shell fills with pearls only when content.
english: The pearl forms where craving has stopped scraping the shell.
spanish: La perla se forma donde el anhelo deja de raspar la concha.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — love heals pride and self-conceit.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — love heals pride and self-conceit.
english: Only a wound deeper than pride can cure pride.
spanish: Sólo una herida más profunda que el orgullo puede curar el orgullo.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — the Beloved is all; the lover is a veil.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — the Beloved is all; the lover is a veil.
english: The seeker is the veil over what is already seeking.
spanish: El buscador es el velo sobre aquello que ya busca.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Whinfield PDF. Public-domain translation witness; short working rendering.

artifact: Masnavi — the mirror does not reflect because rust remains.
original: Masnavi I, Prologue — the mirror does not reflect because rust remains.
english: The face of the Real waits for the mirror to be cleaned.
spanish: El rostro de lo Real espera a que el espejo sea limpiado.
recommendedEditionSource: E. H. Whinfield — Masnavi i Ma'navi, Book I
